key_points: Dorm quality varies wildly; 'Dirty South' (Ewing, Hoover, Wray) consistently rated worst
New South dorms ranked best; renovations underway for traditional dorms over next few years
Bathroom stall doors frequently broken with no locks — reported across multiple buildings
key_quotes: Why do almost none of the bathroom stall doors close? The men's locker room has 4 stalls, only one locks.
Renovations for traditional dorms will take place within the next few years. Dirty South dorms will eventually be renovated.
Got a single dorm in Hoover House... I have heard things about back south being kinda crummy.

key_points: Party scene is dominant and highly visible; non-partiers worry there's nothing else to do
Making genuine friends is genuinely hard — multiple students report years of loneliness
Court Street bar scene is a cultural anchor; Athens welcomed warmly by non-students too
key_quotes: I've been told by some that there isn't much else to do besides party there.
It is my third year at Ohio University and despite meeting many great people I have never made a single friend.
This is my first week and I haven't made any friends... my dorm mate has tons of friends already and is partying every night.

key_points: Nelson dining hall switched to pre-plating all meals — students and staff say it's slow and wasteful
Lots of off-campus restaurants on Court Street compensate for mediocre dining hall food
Student health insurance fee (~$1,300) auto-charged unless you actively waive it
key_quotes: They have been pre-plating all meals. Me and my coworkers think its dumb and needlessly slow.
There's SO many restaurants uptown... Bell Tower is the best.
They were gonna charge me 1.3k on top of my tuition as a commuter — it completely slipped my mind.

key_points: Multiple students report depression and severe loneliness persisting for years at OU
One student medically withdrew after fall semester due to depression; described experience as 'okay at best'
Isolated Appalachian location amplifies mental health struggles for students without strong social networks
key_quotes: I am a senior at OU with no friends. I'm tired of staying in my room and being depressed.
I never, at any point could say 'wow, I'm really enjoying my experience here.' It was okay at best.
I try to be nice to the people in my classes even if ppl can be mean... maybe I'm just genuinely not the social person I thought I was.

key_points: Cutler Scholars and HTC programs offer competitive merit aid worth researching early
OU described as notably more affordable than Ohio State — a major draw for cost-conscious students
One student stuck paying $2,629 loan overage mid-final-semester with threat of class removal
key_quotes: OU was so much more affordable [than OSU]. I'm actually terrified to commit.
I received an email stating I exceeded my undergraduate federal loan limit by $2,629 and need to pay immediately or I will be dropped from my classes.
I'm interested in learning more about the Cutler Awards. From my understanding, it's an honors program with a renewable scholarship.

key_points: Scripps School of Journalism widely recognized as a top program and key reason to choose OU over OSU
Honors Tutorial College (HTC) is a genuine hidden gem — individualized, Oxford-style tutorials
Some students waitlisted for their own required capstone class — scheduling failures affect graduation
key_quotes: I've been admitted into Ohio University's Scripps Journalism program but people have told me there is really nothing to do in Athens.
I'm waitlisted for my CAPSTONE!!! I should be able to schedule without worrying about a class needed to graduate!!!!
Ohio University is home to one of the oldest Music Therapy undergrad degree programs in the US. Right now it's under threat.

key_points: President Lori Gonzalez drew heavy criticism: canceled Black Alumni Week, removed Women's and LGBT centers
Administration spent close to $1M fighting faculty union while citing 'budget cuts' across departments
Academic programs eliminated in 2025 due to SB1 compliance; Music Therapy program facing sunset
key_quotes: We've experienced: cancellation of Black Alumni week, removal of Women's center and LGBT center, spent close to a million fighting the new faculty union.
Mass understaffing across the labor union because of 'budget cuts'... only the students and low-rung workers are suffering.
Ohio University announced the elimination of the following programs to comply with SB1... Music Therapy directed to sunset.

key_points: Athens is a genuine, charming college town — beautiful campus widely praised by students and visitors
Remote Appalachian location means limited job/internship access; you're isolated without a car
Historic campus with strong alumni nostalgia; outdoorsy vibe with proximity to hills, lakes, forests
key_quotes: When I was there it was electric — like being trapped in a golden palace of beer, natural beauty, and attractive students.
I live in Ohio, where public transit isn't really a thing.
I parked at the fairgrounds and walked through the campus... thank you Athens.

key_points: Scripps journalism grads have strong outcomes; Post student newspaper provides real-world experience
Engineering and business programs lack the employer name-recognition of OSU — matters for recruiting
Athens location makes in-person internships rare; students must be proactive about opportunities
key_quotes: On one hand, I've been admitted into Ohio University's Scripps Journalism program... on the other hand, OSU doesn't have as good a journalism program.
I'm committed to OU for mechanical engineering and I'm mostly scared about running out of things to do.
The Paper spinoff main character went to OU... as a Scripps alum, this was very exciting!

key_points: Strong value vs. Ohio State — significantly cheaper with comparable or better programs in journalism/HTC
Out-of-state students report sticker shock; merit scholarships like Cutler can offset costs meaningfully
Faculty union reports faculty are underpaid relative to peer Ohio universities — may affect teaching quality
key_quotes: OU was so much more affordable [than OSU]... I'm committed to OU for mechanical engineering.
Ohio University union pushes for raises, saying faculty underpaid against other Ohio universities.
I transferred to this University but can't afford it. Now I have to stay in my current college studying something I care little about.

key_points: Strong party culture and beautiful campus, but social isolation is a real and recurring problem
Administration actively rolling back DEI resources while faculty push back through unionization
Passionate alumni love for Athens; current students more ambivalent about day-to-day experience
key_quotes: Super liberal but to be expected anymore. Graduated 2019. Miss my friends and the time... Won't regret it.
How is Ohio University so bad at PR?
I can't believe how much I hate and love ohio at the same time. The summers are unmatched, but yet there's nothing here for dreamers like me.

key_points: **Administration**: LGBT/Women's centers removed, Black Alumni Week canceled under current president
**Academics**: Programs eliminated under SB1; Music Therapy being sunsetted; capstone waitlisting seniors
**Campus Safety**: Neo-Nazi speaker invited to campus; police department investigating social media threats
key_quotes: Is Lori Gonzalez our worst president ever? — cancellation of Black Alumni week, removal of Women's center and LGBT center.
Notice: neo-nazi invited to speak on campus tomorrow... this agitator's goal is to collect clips of students and profit off it.
Ohio University fired football head coach Brian Smith after he was accused of having an extramarital affair with an undergraduate student.

key_points: Honors Tutorial College (HTC) offers rare Oxford-style one-on-one tutorials — criminally underrated nationally
Scripps School of Journalism is legitimately elite; The Post student paper gives real professional experience
Glidden Hall music balconies open to all students — free, beautiful space with no major restrictions
key_quotes: Math HTC at OU... Williams College is approximately the same price if not a little cheaper. [Student chose HTC as comparable to Williams for math.]
The Paper spinoff main character went to OU and worked at the school paper. As a Scripps alum, this was very exciting!
A friend and I want to sing on the Glidden balconies... are those doors locked? [Answer: open to everyone.]
